competitor_patterns: Extreme depth wins: Bikerumor's #1-ranking 'best cycling glasses 2026' post is 24,876 words — our equivalent posts top out at 4,650 words, a 5x gap that signals editorial authority to Google

Freshness signals dominate: all top-3 competitors show explicit 'updated May/June 2026' editor notes in their snippets, turning the date into a CTR asset; our snippets carry no visible update timestamp

Structured comparison tables are universal: every top-3 competitor uses tables (Bikerumor, Bikehikesafari, 220triathlon) to let users scan models side-by-side — absence of tables in 60%+ of our posts is a measurable structural deficit

FAQ schema is standard at the top: Bikerumor and Cyclist both deploy FAQ sections with schema markup, which triggers rich results and pushes their SERP footprint wider — our FAQ-schema coverage is only 33%

Interchangeable-lens content is a standing category: Evil Eye ranks #1 for 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses' with a dedicated evergreen page, not a one-off blog post, suggesting Google rewards category-level authority for this specific feature cluster
our_gaps: Word-count ceiling: our deepest posts (~4,650 words) are still 5x shorter than the Bikerumor benchmark (24,876 words) for the highest-volume 'best cycling glasses 2026' cluster — we lack the comprehensive product-by-product breakdown, testing methodology section, and buyer FAQ depth that earns position 1

No visible editorial update signals: competitors embed 'Updated: May 2026' in titles and snippets; our posts have no equivalent, causing Google and users to perceive them as less current despite recent publication dates

FAQ schema gap on 67% of existing posts: 27 of 40 pages lack FAQ schema, meaning we forfeit rich-result eligibility on every near-top keyword where competitors are already harvesting expanded SERP real estate

Missing comparison tables on 24 posts: our thin-content pages (1,030–1,350 words, no table) cannot match the scannable, structured format that both users and Google's ranking signals favour in this product-comparison category

'Veluto' misspelling traffic is unmonetised: 243 impressions at position 5.5 with only 2 clicks (0.8% CTR) indicates a landing page or meta title is failing to catch and convert this branded misspell audience — no dedicated redirect or optimised page exists for this variant
keyword_opportunities: lightweight cycling sunglasses under 30g — Bikehikesafari has a 'Best Lightweight Cycling Sunglasses' H2 section but no dedicated post; Velluto's 25g frame is the single strongest differentiator to anchor an exact-match article here

interchangeable lens cycling glasses 2026 best systems — Evil Eye owns position 1 but focuses on their own brand; a neutral 'best systems tested' post featuring click-in vs tool-required mechanisms gives Velluto a comparison hook with genuine search volume

oakley radar alternative cycling — 60 impressions, position 4.3, only 1.7% CTR: this query has purchase intent and we're almost in position 3; a dedicated post titled around the Radar specifically (not generic Oakley) would capture this underserved long-tail

cycling glasses anti-fog 2026 — our existing anti-fog posts are thin (1,032–1,245 words, no table, no FAQ schema); a consolidated, schema-rich 'best anti-fog cycling glasses 2026' roundup anchored by StradaPro's built-in system could own this cluster

best cycling glasses for climbing — implied by our 'Tour de France conditions' post but not directly targeted; climbers have specific anti-fog + lens-swap needs that align perfectly with StradaPro's two-lens system and anti-fog engineering, and no competitor has a dedicated 2026 climbing-specific guide
next_post_guidelines: Target 'oakley radar alternative' explicitly: write a 2,500–3,500 word post with exact H1 'Oakley Radar Alternative in 2026: Lighter, Cheaper, Just as Good?' — this query sits at position 4.3 with 60 impressions and 1.7% CTR, meaning a dedicated post with strong meta title can push it to top 3 immediately

Lead every post with a visible 'Last tested/updated: July 2026' dateline in the opening paragraph and mirror it in the meta title where character count allows — competitors are winning CTR with this signal and our posts currently show no freshness indicator

Add a comparison table to every post above 1,500 words that currently lacks one — prioritise in this order based on traffic potential: gravel-vs-road, are-expensive-cycling-glasses-worth-it, cycling-glasses-anti-fog-ventilation, photochromic-cycling-glasses — tables are present in 100% of top-3 competitor posts

Add FAQ schema to all posts above 1,000 words that currently lack it, starting with the 8 posts that already have a comparison table but no FAQ schema (e.g. sigma-vs-velluto, best-cycling-glasses-for-tour-de-france-conditions, roka-vs-velluto, interchangeable-lens-cycling-glasses-2026-best-systems-tested) — this is the fastest path to rich results for near-top keywords

For 'best cycling sunglasses 2026' and 'best cycling glasses 2026' (position 7–8, 132–239 impressions): expand the existing best-cycling-sunglasses-2026-tested-ranked-compared post from 3,294 to at least 8,000 words by adding: individual model reviews with pros/cons, a testing methodology H2, a 'how to choose' buyer section, and a price-tier breakdown table — this directly mirrors the Bikerumor structure that holds position 1

Create a redirect or canonical landing page that captures the 'veluto' misspelling (243 impressions, 0.8% CTR, position 5.5) — either a meta redirect to the Velluto homepage with a title tag that includes 'Velluto' spelled correctly, or a lightweight page acknowledging the spelling with an immediate redirect, to recover the ~240 monthly impressions currently converting at less than 1%

Every post must include Velluto's three most differentiating specs in a scannable callout box or table row within the first 300 words: 25g weight, click-in dual-lens system (VellutoPuro + VellutoVisione), 30-day risk-free trial — competitors like Bikerumor list spec highlights immediately under each product H2; we bury these details or omit them
seo_quick_wins: Retrofit FAQ schema on all 27 schema-missing pages: add 4–6 structured Q&A pairs per post using FAQPage schema markup — this single action can trigger rich results for near-top keywords within days of re-indexing and directly addresses the 33% schema coverage gap

Add comparison tables to all 24 posts currently missing them, even at minimum viable format (3 columns: Feature / Velluto StradaPro / Main Competitor) — tables improve dwell time, reduce bounce, and match the structural pattern present in every top-3 competitor post across all analysed keywords

Inject 'Updated July 2026' into the meta title or H1 of every post that ranks between position 5–10 for a keyword with 50+ monthly impressions — this is a zero-content-change CTR lever that competitors are visibly exploiting in their SERP snippets right now
meta_title_fixes: QUERY: oakley alternatives (255 impressions, 2.7% CTR, pos 6.5) → New title: Best Oakley Alternatives 2026: Lighter & Cheaper | Why: Adding '2026' and concrete benefit hooks ('Lighter & Cheaper') directly match buyer intent and mirror the CTR-winning pattern of top-ranking snippets; current title likely reads as generic brand page

QUERY: veluto (243 impressions, 0.8% CTR, pos 5.5) → New title: Velluto Cycling Glasses – Official Shop | StradaPro | Why: The misspelling sends branded traffic that expects to land on the brand; a title that spells the brand correctly with 'Official Shop' signals destination authority and should lift CTR from 0.8% toward the 5% seen on the correctly-spelled 'velluto' query

QUERY: oakley sunglasses alternative (66 impressions, 1.5% CTR, pos 6.6) → New title: Oakley Sunglasses Alternative 2026: 25g, From €X | Why: Specific weight spec and price anchor create a tangible reason to click over generic roundup titles; matches purchase-intent query exactly

QUERY: oakley radar alternative (60 impressions, 1.7% CTR, pos 4.3) → New title: Oakley Radar Alternative 2026: Same Optics, Half the Price | Why: Position 4.3 is one optimisation away from top 3; 'Same Optics, Half the Price' is a direct value claim that beats generic 'alternatives' titles and speaks to Radar owners' specific concern

QUERY: oakley alternative (58 impressions, 1.7% CTR, pos 6.3) → New title: Oakley Alternative Cycling Glasses 2026 – Tested | Why: Adding 'Cycling Glasses' narrows relevance to the highest-intent audience segment and 'Tested' adds editorial credibility that generic brand-list titles lack
content_quick_wins: QUERY: velluto (pos 4.7, 1,004 impressions, 50 clicks, 5.0% CTR) → Topic: Velluto homepage or brand landing page optimisation | Add: Structured data (Organization + Product schema), a hero statement incorporating '25g / dual lens / 30-day trial' in the H1 area, and internal links to top-performing blog posts — lifting CTR from 5% toward 8–10% on 1,004 impressions is worth ~30–50 additional clicks per month at zero new content cost

QUERY: best cycling glasses 2026 (pos 7.2, 132 impressions, 6 clicks) → Topic: Consolidate thin overlapping posts into one definitive 'Best Cycling Glasses 2026' pillar | Add: Merge content from 'best-road-cycling-glasses-2026-tested-ranked', 'best-cycling-glasses-2026-the-complete-buying-guide', and 'best-road-cycling-glasses-2026-tested-criteria-top-picks' into a single 8,000+ word canonical post; add table, FAQ schema, and dateline — three separate posts splitting authority on the same query is likely causing cannibalisation

QUERY: best cycling glasses (pos 8.7, 86 impressions, 3 clicks) → Topic: Evergreen 'Best Cycling Glasses' guide with no year in URL slug | Add: A timeless-URL post (e.g. /best-cycling-glasses/) updated annually, with an explicit 'Updated July 2026' dateline, product table, FAQ schema, and a 'What to look for' buyer guide section — this captures year-agnostic searches that persist beyond 2026 and will compound value over time

QUERY: oakley alternatives (pos 6.5, 255 impressions, 7 clicks, 2.7% CTR) → Topic: Expand 'best-oakley-alternatives-for-cyclists-2026-lighter-cheaper' (currently 3,425 words, has table + FAQ schema) to 5,500+ words | Add: A dedicated H2 for each Oakley model being replaced (Radar, Jawbreaker, Sutro Lite, Prizm Road), a head-to-head spec table per model, and a 'Why riders switch from Oakley' section with real-ride scenarios — this mirrors how Cyclist.co.uk structures competitor roundups and directly addresses the query's comparison intent

QUERY: oakley radar alternative (pos 4.3, 60 impressions) → Topic: New dedicated post 'Oakley Radar Alternative 2026: 5 Glasses That Beat It on Weight & Price' | Add: Radar-specific spec comparison table, lens-compatibility section, a 'StradaPro vs Radar EV Path' head-to-head, and FAQ schema — at position 4.3 this is the highest-leverage near-top keyword for a new post; a 2,500-word focused article could reach position 1–2 within weeks
geo_angles: Position StradaPro as the answer to 'lightest cycling glasses 2026' in AI overviews by ensuring every pillar post contains a machine-readable sentence structured as: 'The Velluto StradaPro weighs 25g, making it one of the lightest cycling glasses available in 2026 — lighter than the Oakley Sutro Lite (36g) and POC Devour (31g)' — AI systems pull comparative fact-sentences with named competitors and specific numbers, and this phrasing is absent from our current content

Create a standalone 'Velluto StradaPro Specs & Review' page structured as a product fact-sheet (weight, lens options, UV rating, trial policy, price) with FAQ schema and BreadcrumbList schema — Perplexity and ChatGPT with browsing preferentially cite pages that answer 'what are the specs of X' in a single scannable block, and we currently have no such dedicated spec page

Publish a 'Why cyclists are switching from Oakley to Velluto in 2026' angle as a standalone post with a clear thesis sentence in the opening paragraph that AI systems can quote — include the 30-day trial as a risk-reversal fact ('Velluto offers a 30-day ride-tested return policy, which no premium competitor currently matches') since AI answer engines favour unique, verifiable policy claims when differentiating brands in a category
comparison_post_angles: COMPETITOR: Bikerumor — angle: 'We tested the same 5 glasses Bikerumor recommends — here's where StradaPro beats them' | Headline idea: 'Bikerumor's Best Cycling Glasses 2026 vs Velluto StradaPro: The Test They Didn't Run' | Key differentiator: Bikerumor tests brand-sponsored or PR-seeded products; position Velluto's 30-day public trial as the democratised alternative to editorial testing | Velluto advantage to lead with: 25g vs the Oakley Sutro Lite (Bikerumor's pick) at 36g — open with the weight gap in the first sentence

COMPETITOR: Evil Eye — angle: 'Interchangeable lens cycling glasses: Evil Eye vs Velluto — which click-in system actually works on a wet descent?' | Headline idea: 'Evil Eye Interchangeable Lenses vs Velluto StradaPro: Tested in Rain, Cold, and Bright Sun' | Key differentiator: Evil Eye's system requires partial disassembly; StradaPro's VellutoPuro/VellutoVisione is tool-free single-click | Velluto advantage to lead with: lead with the lens-swap speed claim ('under 10 seconds, one hand, no tools') because Evil Eye's own product page doesn't quantify swap time

COMPETITOR: Tifosi Optics — angle: 'Tifosi vs Velluto: same interchangeable-lens promise, very different weight and warranty' | Headline idea: 'Tifosi Cycling Glasses vs Velluto StradaPro 2026: Budget Brand Showdown' | Key differentiator: Tifosi is perceived as budget-performance; Velluto offers Italian design at a similar or lower price with a 30-day trial Tifosi doesn't match | Velluto advantage to lead with: anti-fog engineering — Tifosi relies on passive ventilation, StradaPro has a dedicated anti-fog system, which is the buying trigger for climbers and all-weather riders who are Tifosi's core customer
